full_prompt: Design a single-piece structural-steel bridge truss for a collegiate bridge-design event. The bridge shall span a nominal 20 ft (6.10 m) clear gap and carry a maximum applied vertical load of 2,500 lb (11,120 N) over the decking at the worst-case loading location. Exactly two stringers carry the decking loads. Overall bridge envelope: 6.5 m long (to allow support bearings outside the 20 ft span), 1.0 m wide (stringer-to-stringer), 0.7 m tall. Bridge is a through-truss welded from A500 Grade B steel hollow structural sections (HSS). Material: ASTM A500 Grade B steel, E = 200 GPa, yield = 315 MPa, ultimate = 400 MPa, density = 7850 kg/m^3. Primary members: top chord HSS 50 x 50 x 3 mm, bottom chord HSS 50 x 50 x 3 mm, diagonal web HSS 40 x 40 x 3 mm, transverse floor beams HSS 40 x 40 x 3 mm. Nodes are welded (modeled as rigid connections). Load cases: (R1) Service load: 2500 lb (11,120 N) concentrated at mid-span on both stringers simultaneously; maximum midspan vertical deflection of the loaded stringers <= span/500 = 6100 / 500 = 12.2 mm. (R2) Stress pass/fail: under R1, maximum von Mises stress in any member <= yield / 1.67 (LRFD phi 0.9 / gamma 1.5 equivalent) = 189 MPa. (R3) Linear buckling: under factored load 1.5 x 11,120 = 16,680 N, the first buckling eigenvalue (compressive chord / diagonal buckling) shall be >= 2.5. (R4) Bridge total mass (stiffness / lightness scoring): aim for minimum; hard cap mass <= 120 kg. (R5) Lateral sway under a horizontal rubbing load of 50 lb (222 N) applied at mid-span top chord <= 12 mm. (R6) Construction constraint: the bridge shall be assembled from members no longer than 1.2 m (transportation constraint) - this is a geometry rule not analysis, but bolted splice locations must carry the full R1 load. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static) + linear buckling. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded assembly (treated as a single structural idealization for FEA), member schedule, and a report listing total mass, maximum midspan deflection, max von Mises stress, first buckling load factor, lateral sway.
short_prompt:
Design a welded A500 Grade B steel truss bridge for a collegiate bridge-design event, spanning a 20 ft (6.10 m) clear gap and carrying a maximum vertical load of 2500 lb (11,120 N) at the worst-case deck location through exactly two stringers. Sizing follows an LRFD-style factoring (phi 0.9 / gamma 1.5 equivalent) on A500 Grade B yield. Nodes welded and modelled as rigid.

Envelope: overall 6.5 m long (support bearings outside the 20 ft span) x 1.0 m wide (stringer-to-stringer) x 0.7 m tall. Primary members HSS 50 x 50 x 3 mm (chords) and HSS 40 x 40 x 3 mm (diagonals / floor beams). Construction rule: no single member longer than 1.2 m (splices must still carry full service load).

Load cases.
- LC1 service load: 11,120 N at midspan on both stringers simultaneously; midspan stringer deflection <= span/500 = 12.2 mm.
- LC2 factored buckling: 1.5 x 11,120 = 16,680 N, first buckling eigenvalue check on compression chords / diagonals.
- LC3 lateral rub: 50 lbf (222 N) horizontal at midspan top chord, lateral sway <= 12 mm.

prompt.title: Collegiate steel through-truss bridge (20 ft clear span)
prompt.brief: HSS steel truss 20 ft clear span, 2 stringers, 11.12 kN vertical load. Deflection <= L/500 and buckling >= 2.5.

load_cases.name: service_vertical
load_cases.direction: vertical at midspan on both stringers

boundary_conditions.mounting: pinned at one end, roller at other (simple span)
boundary_conditions.loads: concentrated vertical or horizontal at midspan
